items.title: Super Mario Galaxy, Project Hail Mary help support best box office since start of pandemic
items.summary_auto: A string of box office successes is, perhaps surprisingly, supported by what seemed a fading remnant of the Hollywood landscape just a few years ago: non-franchises and even the occasional original feature.

items.title: Judge throws out Trump's $10B US lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein birthday card
items.summary_auto: A federal judge on Monday dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump’s defamation lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal, a setback for Trump in his legal campaign against media companies he accuses of treating him unfairly.

items.title: B.C. NDP caucus split over premier's plan to amend Indigenous reconciliation framework law
items.summary_auto: The NDP government is courting two Independent MLAs to support the premier’s controversial plan to suspend sections of the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous People’s Act. Sources tell CBC News a growing number of NDP MLAs oppose the changes the premier is pushing.

items.title: Ontario capping number of school trustees per board, creating new oversight roles
Alberta's access restrictions threaten privacy and democratic role of libraries, critics say
items.summary_auto: Critics of Alberta's proposed rule changes to public libraries say the true danger lies in giving the province power to dictate access and potentially snoop on which books people are checking out.

items.title: What's next for Hungary after 16 years of Orbán?
items.summary_auto: The morning after a dramatic election result, Hungarians are reacting to the end of Viktor Orbán's 16 years in power. Questions remain about how the new government, led by Péter Magyar, will shake things up.

items.title: Cross-country tour to celebrate FIFA World Cup to wrap with stop in Niagara Falls in July
items.summary_auto: FIFA recently announced Niagara Falls will be a final stop in an upcoming cross-country tour to celebrate the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

items.title: At least 3 killed in Israeli strike on Gaza as talks to advance ceasefire back on
items.summary_auto: An Israeli airstrike killed at least three Palestinians in the Gaza Strip on Monday, health officials said, as mediators met leaders from Hamas in an effort to shore up a U.S.-brokered ceasefire deal.

items.title: Young adult cancer survivors at twice the risk of later cancers, study suggests
items.summary_auto: Survivors of cancer in adolescence and young adulthood are at increased risk for nearly all types later, Canadian researchers find.

items.title: EU president calls Hungary election a 'victory for fundamental freedoms' as world leaders congratulate Magyar
items.summary_auto: European Union ​Commission President Ursula von der Leyen hailed Hungary's election as "a victory for fundamental freedoms," and compared the ousting of nationalist Viktor Orbán to the country's 1956 ‌anti-Soviet uprising and its 1989 break with communism.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Highlights from Coachella
items.summary_auto: Over the weekend, fans flocked to the Californian desert for the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival for performances by top global artists and emerging acts. Check out some of the scenes from the first weekend.

items.title: Thousands of farms are 'boarded shut' due to 'outdated' tax rules, Ontario farmer says
items.summary_auto: Farmers in southern Ontario are fighting for a change to the Income Tax Act, so that inheriting nieces and nephews are included in tax deferrals. They say it will help keep farms across Canada open.

items.title: Thousands of farms are 'boarded shut' due to 'outdated' tax rules, says Ontario farmer
items.summary_auto: Farmers in southern Ontario are fighting for a change to the Income Tax Act, so that inheriting nieces and nephews are included in tax deferrals. They say it will help keep farms across Canada open.

items.title: Ghanaian Premier League player killed in suspected armed robbery attack on team bus
items.summary_auto: Dominic Frimpong of Ghanaian Premier League club Berekum Chelsea was killed after the team's bus was attacked by a group of suspected armed robbers, the country's soccer association said Monday.

items.title: Israel launches attack to seize Lebanon border town ahead of U.S.-hosted talks
items.summary_auto: Israeli troops launched an attack to seize a key south Lebanon town from Hezbollah fighters holed up inside on Monday, pressing the war on the Iran-backed group on the eve of historic talks between Israeli and Lebanese government envoys.

items.title: Russian swimmers to be allowed to compete with flag and anthem after restrictions dropped
items.summary_auto: The governing body for international swimming and aquatic sports will allow athletes from Russia to compete without restrictions and with their national flag and anthem.

items.title: How would a U.S. military blockade of Iran's ports work?
items.summary_auto: CBC's David Common explains what's expected around the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. military said it would move to block ships travelling to or from Iran's ports.

items.title: Eric Swalwell suspends California governor campaign amid sex assault allegation
items.summary_auto: Democratic House Rep. Eric Swalwell suspended his campaign for California governor on Sunday following sexual assault allegations that he continues to deny.

items.title: Oil prices rise above $100, North American stock market holds steady as U.S.-Iran tensions continue
items.summary_auto: Brent crude oil, the international standard, hovered around $100 US a barrel on Monday — up from roughly $70 per barrel before the war in late February. North American markets were mostly flat as of midday, after dipping earlier in the morning.

items.title: Foe by Iain Reid 1st book to be eliminated on Canada Reads 2026
Iran port blockade has started, Trump says, after pledging approaching attack ships will be 'eliminated'
Alberta testing AI-powered drone technology in battle against wild boar
items.summary_auto: Researchers are turning to a new high-tech combination of drones, thermal cameras and artificial intelligence to get a new vantage point on Alberta’s wild boar problem.

items.title: Britney Spears enters treatment facility weeks after DUI arrest
items.summary_auto: Britney Spears has entered a substance abuse treatment facility just over a month after she was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs.

items.title: Conservation groups call for 'science-based' approach to Alberta grizzly bear hunt proposal
items.summary_auto: Conservation groups say recent calls for the provincial government to legalize the hunting of threatened grizzly bears in Alberta aren't justified by science due to a lack of up-to-date population studies.

items.title: Lawsuit accuses Winnipeg woman of taking $6M from non-profit to fund vacations, buy TikTok coins
items.summary_auto: A non-profit organization — once in charge of millions of dollars in federal funds — is suing a former employee, alleging she misappropriated more than $6 million to pay for vacations and divert to a Jamaican dance hall singer.

items.title: Edmonton police emails, documents provide new information on Canada-first AI facial recognition bodycam pilot
items.summary_auto: Key details include a “critical fault” system outage, possible extension of pilot project and that facial recognition model was supplied by Corsight AI, a company whose technology has reportedly been used for mass surveillance in Gaza.

items.title: Strikes on alleged drug boats kill 5, leave 1 survivor in eastern Pacific, U.S. military says
items.summary_auto: The U.S. military said Sunday that it blew up two boats accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing a total of five people and leaving one survivor, as the Trump administration pursues its campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America while preparing a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: This First Nation's community in N.S. is asking if it wants pot sold on the reserve at all
items.summary_auto: Police raids of Indigenous-owned cannabis stores in Nova Scotia have been headline news. But that’s overshadowing other questions: whether band members even want cannabis stores, and if so, who should own and profit from them. That’s what band members are being asked on the Glooscap First Nation.

items.title: This N.S. First Nations community asking if it wants pot sold on the reserve at all
items.summary_auto: Police raids of Indigenous-owned cannabis stores in Nova Scotia have been headline news. But that’s overshadowing other questions: whether band members even want cannabis stores, and if so, who should own and profit from them. That’s what band members are being asked on the Glooscap First Nation.

items.title: As the N.L. newsprint industry sputters, these sawmill owners are framing up a bright future
items.summary_auto: The future of the newsprint mill in Corner Brook is about as murky as the slurry used to make paper. And all that uncertainty is also being felt by the saw milling sector.

items.title: Cancer remains top cause of death in Canada as new study shows multiple types rising
Shoppers Drug Mart gave her the wrong medication. Months later, she landed in the ER
Pressure ramps up on Ford government to address rising cost of living
items.summary_auto: As the cost of living rises in the province, Ontario’s government finds itself under increasing pressure to address affordability just weeks after it delivered its latest budget. Opposition parties and anti-poverty advocates are questioning the wisdom of holding off on immediate measures to provide cost of living relief.

items.title: Family of Sudbury couple killed in latest Highway 69 crash urges Ontario to honour expansion promise
items.summary_auto: Family and friends of a couple killed last week on a stretch of Highway 69 that's seen other fatal crashes are questioning why the Ontario government has yet to deliver on a long-promised expansion from two to four lanes. Vinod and Shilpa Patel died in a collision along the only remaining two-lane stretch of the highway, near Britt.

items.title: Voters head to polls in 3 federal byelections that could deliver majority Liberal government
items.summary_auto: Voters in two Ontario ridings and one in Quebec will head to the polls on Monday for a slate of crucial federal byelections that could deliver the Liberals a majority government — cementing their hold on power for the next few years.

items.title: How McGill’s new greenhouse will help grow more climate change-resistant agriculture
items.summary_auto: McGill University’s Macdonald Campus has a new greenhouse, which features seven bays, a classroom and demonstration rooms. Staff explain how they plan to use these new facilities.

items.title: Trump attacks Pope Leo, then draws backlash for Christ-like AI post
items.summary_auto: Pope Leo told Reuters on Monday that he plans to continue speaking out against war after U.S. ‌President Donald Trump's direct attack the previous day on the leader of the 1.4-billion-member church.

items.title: Rory repeats: McIlroy goes back-to-back at the Masters to join Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo and Tiger Woods
items.summary_auto: Rory McIlroy joined more elite company Sunday at the Masters when he pulled away with a pair of birdies around Amen Corner and, as usual, saved a little drama for the end before taking his place in Augusta National history as only the fourth back-to-back champion.
